task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite dashboard UI is visible with the title 'Post-Incident Review' displayed at the top.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Hourly Peak CPU' is created with the correct targets
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look across screenshots for a graph titled 'Hourly Peak CPU'. The target configuration should show aliasByNode and summarize functions applied to the three EC2 instance CPU utilization metrics.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Gap-Filled Temperature' is created with the correct target
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look across screenshots for a graph titled 'Gap-Filled Temperature'. The target configuration should show the keepLastValue function applied to the datacenter machine temperature metric.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Request Rate Summary' is created with the correct target
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look across screenshots for a graph titled 'Request Rate Summary'. The target configuration should show alias, summarize, and derivative functions applied to the load balancer requests count metric.
A notification (e.g., 'Dashboard Saved') or a UI state indicating that the dashboard has been saved to the Graphite server.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web UI to create the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Graphite web interface in a browser, not just terminal commands or API scripts.
The graphs in the dashboard should display plotted lines or data points, indicating they are successfully querying real time-series data.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected answers into a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs and titles must be entered into the actual Graphite dashboard input fields, not a notepad or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Post-Incident Review'. The dashboard must contain exactly three graphs with the following exact titles: 'Hourly Peak CPU', 'Gap-Filled Temperature', and 'Request Rate Summary'.
